name: Marty's Pizza Dough
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Water, lukewarm (105 to 115 Degrees Fahrenheit)
recipeInstructions:
1. Put flour into a large bowl and make a deep well in the middle.
2. Pour warm water into the well, mix in the yeast using a bit of flour in the mix.
3. Add sugar, salt, and flour to the well; let stand for 10 minutes until mixture bubbles.
4. Slowly stir all of the flour into the yeast mixture.
5. When it gets too thick to handle, place on a board and knead until elastic.
6. Form a ball and place into a greased bowl; turn over so the top is greased.
7. Cover with a towel and let rise in a warm, draft-free place for an hour or until doubled in bulk.
8. Preheat oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit.
9. Punch down dough and divide in half. Refrigerate one half for later (allow extra time to rise).
10. Roll the other half into a circle about 15 inches across.
11. Place on a greased pizza pan; roll edges to form a rim.
12. Spread half the sauce on the pizza pie (see recipe), top with shredded mozzarella, and other favorite toppings (mushrooms, olives, pepperoni, etc).
13. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes until edges are brown and cheese is bubbly.
14. Cut each pie into 6 wedges.
